reason: Initial green tests did not prove the required validity and reconstruction invariants.
The shared discovery prompt required questions.json without exposing its required schema; the first smoke attempt produced useful but schema-incompatible questions.
Single-topic indexing missed exact founder facts for broad high-value questions, and Lamina init combined canonical headings required by its existing validator.

The preserved third smoke attempt showed that Lamina verification correctly records evidence-backed findings in run.json, which conflicted with the benchmark requirement that the authoritative post-finalization contract remain byte-immutable.

The fourth preserved smoke attempt passed the main loop and transfer tests but was rejected because the harness-provided filename implementation-contract.json was itself classified as leaked harness content when the fresh builder mentioned it in product documentation.
The fifth preserved smoke attempt was rejected after review because a no-op collaboration wait event with no receiver threads was incorrectly counted as a spawned subagent.

The sixth preserved smoke attempt passed the main build/fix gates and transfer build/test/snapshot gates, then transfer review tried to resume the builder thread inside a separate review runtime home where that thread could not exist. Main review also prepared but did not use its separate runtime home.

The first matched-pilot structured attempt passed the supplied JSON schema but was rejected by an undisclosed semantic rule requiring every transition endpoint to exactly match a state declared on the referenced entity.

The replacement matched-pilot structured attempt passed the supplied JSON schema and draft semantic gate, then final validation imposed undisclosed ready-to-build trace breadth, scenario coverage, and operation-effect requirements. Stopping its paired raw attempt also showed that an interrupt could leave only a started ledger event.

The third raw pilot review kept every application source and configuration file byte-identical but reran the supplied visual checks, regenerating only artifacts/desktop.png and artifacts/mobile.png. The guard incorrectly classified those generated screenshots as application-source edits.

The completed matched pilot showed that Lamina's implementation projection omitted critical trusted-system and UX contract detail, every persona review was broadened by unrelated critical nodes, Codex cumulative session usage was misread as per-phase usage, and one product test pass did not expose a post-identity-transition race.

Post-hardening GPT-5.6 Sol smoke attempt 11 passed the repaired product's 12-test suite including Chromium, then a provider-capacity response during a final browser recheck caused the entire cell to fail. The attempt also showed that a declared check script was not part of independent validation.

The fully completed GPT-5.6 Sol matched development pilot showed that fresh transfer builders could replace explicit trusted, private, durable, or transactional boundaries with browser-owned state, while the claimability audit found implicit result sweeping, non-file manifest hashing, unverified unblinding, and no implemented frozen automated judge.

Structured GPT-5.6 Sol attempt 6 passed check, build, and three test replays but named benchmark-contract.json in README.md. The snapshot auditor correctly rejected the arm leak, while the implementation prompt had not disclosed the product-output hygiene rule.

Structured GPT-5.6 Sol attempt 7 left all pre-existing product files byte-identical and wrote evidence-backed findings to review-findings.md, but its prompt supplied no canonical filename while the runner silently recognized only a legacy allowlist and classified the new report as product source.

The first complete final-hash GPT-5.6 Sol matrix produced a valid 15-artifact package, but a pre-rating content audit found lamina_init, oracle filenames, reviewer identities, review-hypothesis labels, and harness paths in judge-visible native contracts. No judge saw the package and no rating was produced.

The replacement GPT-5.6 Sol matrix completed all three arms, but the blinder refused manifest issuance because oracle_fact survived sanitization even though the broader scanner correctly detected it. No manifest, blind key, rating, or judge exposure occurred.

Two consecutive Lamina GPT-5.6 Sol attempts under the same protocol completed contract, persona review, finalization, implementation, and product tests before the isolated product reviewer was blocked by provider cybersecurity moderation while checking normal authorization correctness. Neither attempt produced an accepted review handoff or quality outcome, and the second reviewer had passed all 22 tests before adversarial actor labels triggered the interruption.

All three isolated GPT-5.6 Sol product reviews completed on their first attempts, confirming the neutral review-language fix. Structured reran its browser tests without editing application source, but the tests created data/e2e.sqlite and SQLite WAL/SHM companions that the review guard misclassified as product-source edits even though it already excluded Playwright's generated last-run record.

A fresh Raw GPT-5.6 Sol attempt passed contract, implementation, build, and three test replays, and its isolated reviewer produced the canonical handoff without editing application source. Rerunning product tests updated the already-existing generated .test-data/care-state.json file, which the guard still treated as authored source.

A fresh matched GPT-5.6 Sol matrix cleared both review-guard regressions, but the hosting goal turn renewed and terminated the attached matrix without delivering a catchable signal. The three preserved attempts therefore retained started events without terminal ledger events.

A detached GPT-5.6 Sol matrix ran agents successfully through the read-only isolated Codex 0.144.6 mount, but Raw's final telemetry gate rejected all phases because CLI version probing depended on the parent user-service PATH, which omitted the host CLI symlink directory.

A complete GPT-5.6 Sol development matrix and its verified 15-artifact GPT-5.5 secondary rating showed a Lamina transfer lead but no overall lead because key graph field and actor-entry boundaries were under-specified. The requested GPT-5.5 execution replication then rejected a Raw transfer review that changed only generated __pycache__ bytecode while leaving application and test source byte-identical.

The first replacement GPT-5.6 Sol Structured contract followed the supplied neutral schema, but the schema and prompt did not expose the newly enforced critical actor entry, entity attribute, and dependency fulfillment fields, so shared semantic validation rejected the schema-conforming artifact before review or implementation.

The complete matched GPT-5.5 matrix produced a verified 15-artifact package, but the first isolated secondary-judge turn created only SQLite SHM/WAL companions while running existing tests and the whole-copy hash misclassified those generated files as authored source edits.

Complete GPT-5.6 Sol and GPT-5.5 development matrices plus 30 accepted blinded secondary ratings missed the requested 90-point target and exposed cross-timezone conversion, recurring lifecycle, runnable identity posture, visible who/when attribution, truthful delivery, and out-of-scope judge calibration gaps.
